Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input
When combobox widget requested, use custom listbox - native select alone is insufficient
At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value
Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real personio-style job application (iframe-hosted, small, tier=high). Widgets: react-select, combobox, location-typeahead. Required: Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute Required: Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input Required: When combobox widget requested, use custom listbox - native select alone is insufficient Required: At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value Required: Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span Reference DOM (Question number prefixes): Fields prefixed with Q7. Q12. style question numbers Reference DOM (Fields inside details accordion): Cover letter and visa fields hidden inside closed details element Reference DOM (Emoji unicode labels): Labels contain flag emoji and unicode; Swedish field marked required Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: label-wraps-deep, div-role-textbox, native-select-not-combobox, readonly-prefill, aria-labelledby-hidden. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: sidebar + main split apply layout. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Question number prefixes; Fields inside details accordion; Emoji unicode labels.
